item.title: Michelle W Bowman: A framework for practical monetary policy decision making
item.speaker: Michelle W Bowman

rating.summary: Governor Bowman outlines her personal framework for monetary policy decision-making, covering conditions for rate cuts, hikes, and holds, while applying it to current conditions as of mid-2026. She notes the U.S. labor market remains fragile, headline PCE inflation has risen to 3.8% largely due to energy prices from the Iran conflict, but underlying core inflation excluding one-off factors remains near 2%. She signals support for holding the current moderately restrictive stance while looking through temporary energy-driven inflation, contingent on the conflict remaining short-lived.
rating.stance_rationale: Bowman leans slightly hawkish by maintaining a moderately restrictive stance and flagging that persistent energy/oil price effects could shift her balance-of-risks assessment toward tightening, but her willingness to look through temporary inflation shocks and her prior dissent in favor of a cut temper any strong hawkish signal.
key_quotes: For now, our moderately restrictive policy stance is intended to help maintain stable labor market conditions while allowing inflation to resume its downward trend toward 2 percent once the effects of tariffs and oil prices dissipate.

The more persistent higher oil prices are-or if we start to see broader effects of higher energy prices on PCE inflation-the more likely I will consider shifting my approach to thinking about the balance of risks.
